title: Quick check: End of manual bidding

questions.prompt: Why does Smart Bidding outperform manual CPC for most accounts?

choices.text: Per-impression bid optimization. Manual sets one bid per keyword applied to every auction. Smart Bidding sets a different bid for each auction based on user-level context — query intent, device, time, audience signals, conversion history, browse behavior — and uses ML to predict conversion probability per impression. Plus it uses signals manual can't access at the per-auction level. Most clean migrations report 15-30% efficiency improvements after the learning phase settles.

questions.explanation: Per-impression bidding with rich signal beats one-bid-per-keyword.

questions.prompt: Account has been on manual CPC for years. Migrating to Smart Bidding. What sequence?

choices.text: Audit conversion tracking first (Smart Bidding only works on clean signal). Record 30-day baseline CPA. Switch to Maximize Conversions for 14 days; note new CPA. Switch to tCPA at the new CPA +10%, expect 7-14 day learning. Then tighten the target in 10-15% steps every 14 days until you find the efficient frontier. Jumping straight to tight tCPA combines learning-phase chaos with an aggressive target — bad early data and worse decisions.

questions.explanation: Tracking → Max Conversions → tCPA at baseline +10% → tighten in steps.
